raw_consumer_statement:
What Happened I am filing this complaint against American Express for failing to properly investigate my billing dispute under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1666 ) and Regulation Z ( 12 C.F.R. 1026.13 ). 

On XX/XX/year>, I was charged {$540.00} by a foreign merchant, XXXX XXXX XXXX, for items valued at {$360.00}. The difference represented undisclosed customs duties and import fees that were not disclosed prior to purchase. My claim is based on material nondisclosure in violation of Federal Trade Commission ( FTC ) pricing regulations, which require sellers to disclose all material pricing terms before checkout. 

XXXX Failure to Investigate I submitted detailed documentation showing that : The merchant did not disclose customs duties at checkout ; Their claim that duties included appeared only after purchase on an invoice ; I provided screenshots of the old checkout flow showing no such disclosure ; I also provided new screenshots showing that the merchant has since updated their website to add a visible line item for XXXX before purchase effectively admitting the prior nondisclosure ; I included an email chain showing I attempted to resolve the issue directly with the merchant. 

Despite this, American Express repeatedly ruled in the merchants favor, relying solely on the same post-sale invoice the merchant previously submitted. AmEx ignored my newly submitted evidence, failed to address my legal claims, and did not conduct a reasonable investigation as required under the FCBA.

Violations and Concerns AmExs handling of my dispute violates 15 U.S.C. 1666 ( a ), which requires a good-faith investigation and a written explanation of findings.

AmEx failed to review or consider my additional evidence submitted on multiple occasions. 

The dispute processors relied on a non-compliant document from the merchant that does not satisfy FTC disclosure rules.

AmExs repeated closure of the dispute without substantive review shows a systemic failure to comply with the FCBAs investigation requirements.

What I Am Requesting That the CFPB direct American Express to reopen and properly investigate the dispute ; That AmEx issue a full refund of {$540.00} ; That AmExs compliance department review this as a potential Fair Credit Billing Act violation ; That AmEx provide written confirmation that future disputes involving foreign merchants will receive full evidence review when new information is submitted. 

Supporting Documents Submitted Letter to AmEx dated XX/XX/year> summarizing website change evidence Screenshots of old and new XXXX checkout pages Original invoice showing bundled duties in product prices Email chain documenting direct refund request to merchant Timeline of all correspondence and escalations
